headline: eToro launches Tori AI companion and Alpha Portfolios — AI-driven social investing goes mainstream
the_so_what: eToro packaging AI-powered quantitative strategies (Alpha Portfolios) alongside a conversational AI companion for 40 million users across 75 countries means hedge-fund-grade analytics are now available to retail investors at platform scale — a commoditisation of research that took Bloomberg three decades to achieve for institutional clients, delivered here in a single product cycle for retail.
summary: eToro launched two AI capabilities for its 40 million registered users across 75 countries. Tori is eToro's AI companion that answers questions, surfaces personalised insights, and guides users across the platform — it does not provide investment advice but offers educational content and market insights. Alongside Tori, eToro launched seven Alpha Portfolios — AI-powered investment strategies built using advanced analysis of eToro's proprietary retail trading data, designed to give retail investors access to quantitative approaches previously available only through hedge funds. eToro also opened an AI API marketplace enabling users to build and publish customised trading tools.

key_stat.label: Alpha Portfolio AI strategies launched — built on eToro's proprietary retail trading data (Aug 2025)

verified_claims: eToro launched Tori AI companion — 'answering questions, surfacing personalised insights, guiding them across the platform' — GlobeNewswire, Aug 7, 2025
Tori does NOT provide investment advice — offers educational content and market insights only — GlobeNewswire
Seven Alpha Portfolio strategies launched — built using eToro's proprietary retail trading data — GlobeNewswire
Alpha Portfolios provide retail investors access to quantitative approaches previously only available through hedge funds — GlobeNewswire
AI API marketplace launched enabling users to build and publish customised tools — GlobeNewswire
eToro has 40 million registered users across 75 countries — GlobeNewswire
fabricated_claims: Original entry claimed '$31B in assets under management' — not present in source; removed in 2026-03-24 revision
Original entry claimed 'Alpha Portfolios continuously rebalance based on market signals' — rebalancing mechanism not described in source; removed
_governance.notes: Source fetched and verified 2026-03-24 via GlobeNewswire. All claims updated to match press release. Original pipeline entry had unverified feature claims and a $31B AUM figure not present in source; corrected via direct fetch.
